records.ety: [OE. candel, candel, AS, candel, fr. L. candela a (white) light made of wax or tallow, fr. candëre to be white. See Candid, and cf. Chandler, Cannel, Kindle.]

defs: A slender, cylindrical body of tallow, containing a wick composed of loosely twisted linen of cotton threads, and used to furnish light.
quotes: How far that little candle throws his beams! So shines a good deed in a naughty world.
notes: ☞ Candles are usually made by repeatedly dipping the wicks in the melted tallow, etc. (“dipped candles”), or by casting or running in a mold.

defs: That which gives light; a luminary.
quotes: By these blessed candles of the night.
